### 🔴 Critical (11)

<a id="t-002"></a><a id="f-002"></a>
#### F-002 · Hardcoded Cryptographic Key

**Severity:** 🔴 Critical - secret committed to the public source repo - extractable on clone, no prior access needed  ·  **Component:** [C-01](#c-01) - Express REST API Backend  ·  **Location:** `lib/insecurity.ts`:26

**Issue:** The RSA private key used to sign all JWT authentication tokens is hardcoded as a string literal in `lib/insecurity.ts`. Anyone with access to the source code (public GitHub repository) can use this key to sign JWT tokens as any user, including admin, and authenticate without a valid password.

**Root cause:** Authentication can be circumvented or forged because credentials, signing keys, or password hashes are weak, missing, or exposed.

**Evidence:** ✓ verified - A signing key is embedded as a literal constant in source.

**Fix:** Move the cryptographic key out of source control into a managed secret store and rotate it → ❶ [M-003](#m-003) — Hardcoded RSA private key enables JWT forgery lib/insecurity.ts

**Classification:** Cryptographic Failures · [CWE-321](https://cwe.mitre.org/data/definitions/321.html) · [OWASP A02:2021](https://owasp.org/Top10/A02_2021/) · walkthrough [Walkthrough §3.2](#32-hardcoded-rsa-private-key-enables-jwt-forgery-libinsecurit)

<a id="t-001"></a><a id="f-001"></a>
#### F-001 · SQL Injection

**Severity:** 🔴 Critical  ·  **Component:** [C-01](#c-01) - Express REST API Backend  ·  **Location:** `routes/login.ts`:37

**Issue:** The login handler constructs a raw SQL query using string interpolation of user-supplied email and password hash values. An attacker can use SQL injection payloads (e.g. `' OR '1'='1'--`) in the email field to bypass authentication entirely and gain access as any user, including admin.

**Root cause:** User input flows into a server-side interpreter (SQL, NoSQL, XML, YAML, LDAP, OS shell) without parameterisation or schema validation.

**Evidence:** ✓ verified - SQL is assembled via string concatenation/interpolation of untrusted input.

```typescript
// routes/login.ts:37
    models.sequelize.query(`SELECT * FROM Users WHERE email = '${req.body.email || ''}' AND password = '${security.hash(req.body.password || '')}' AND deletedAt IS NULL`, { model: UserModel, plain: tr
      .then((authenticatedUser) => { // vuln-code-snippet neutral-line loginAdminChallenge loginBenderChallenge loginJimChallenge
        const user = utils.queryResultToJson(authenticatedUser)
        if (user.data?.id && user.data.totpSecret !== '') {
          res.status(401).json({
            status: 'totp_token_required',
            data: {
```

**Fix:** Switch all SQL execution to parameterised queries or ORM-bound parameters → ❶ [M-001](#m-001) — SQL injection in login routes/login.ts

**Classification:** Injection · [CWE-89](https://cwe.mitre.org/data/definitions/89.html) · [OWASP A03:2021](https://owasp.org/Top10/A03_2021/) · walkthrough [Walkthrough §3.1](#31-sql-injection-in-login-routeslogints)

<a id="t-003"></a><a id="f-003"></a>
#### F-003 · Use of a Broken or Risky Cryptographic Algorithm

**Severity:** 🔴 Critical  ·  **Component:** [C-01](#c-01) - Express REST API Backend  ·  **Location:** `package.json`:1

**Issue:** express-jwt version 0.1.3 is vulnerable to JWT algorithm confusion attacks (`CVE-2015-9235` class). An attacker can craft a JWT with `alg: none` and an empty signature, which will be accepted by `express-jwt@0.1.3` without signature verification. This bypasses authentication entirely.

**Root cause:** Authentication can be circumvented or forged because credentials, signing keys, or password hashes are weak, missing, or exposed.

**Evidence:** ✓ verified - A broken or non-password cryptographic primitive is configured on this path.

**Fix:** Replace the broken algorithm with a vetted modern primitive (AES-GCM / Argon2id / Ed25519) → ❶ [M-004](#m-004) — JWT algorithm none bypass via express jwt lib/insecurity.ts

**Classification:** Cryptographic Failures · [CWE-327](https://cwe.mitre.org/data/definitions/327.html) · [OWASP A02:2021](https://owasp.org/Top10/A02_2021/) · walkthrough [Walkthrough §3.3](#33-jwt-algorithm-none-bypass-via-express-jwt-libinsecurityts)

<a id="t-005"></a><a id="f-005"></a>
#### F-005 · SQL Injection

**Severity:** 🔴 Critical  ·  **Component:** [C-01](#c-01) - Express REST API Backend  ·  **Location:** `routes/search.ts`:23

**Issue:** The search endpoint constructs a raw SQL LIKE query with unescaped user input. An attacker can use UNION-based SQL injection to extract the full database schema and all user data from the SQLite database without authentication.

**Root cause:** User input flows into a server-side interpreter (SQL, NoSQL, XML, YAML, LDAP, OS shell) without parameterisation or schema validation.

**Evidence:** ✓ verified - SQL is assembled via string concatenation/interpolation of untrusted input.

```typescript
// routes/search.ts:23
  return (req: Request, res: Response, next: NextFunction) => {
    let criteria: any = req.query.q === 'undefined' ? '' : req.query.q ?? ''
    criteria = (criteria.length <= 200) ? criteria : criteria.substring(0, 200)
    models.sequelize.query(`SELECT * FROM Products WHERE ((name LIKE '%${criteria}%' OR description LIKE '%${criteria}%') AND deletedAt IS NULL) ORDER BY name`) // vuln-code-snippet vuln-line unionSql
      .then(([products]: any) => {
        const dataString = JSON.stringify(products)
        if (challengeUtils.notSolved(challenges.unionSqlInjectionChallenge)) { // vuln-code-snippet hide-start
```

**Fix:** Switch all SQL execution to parameterised queries or ORM-bound parameters → ❶ [M-002](#m-002) — SQL injection in product search routes/search.ts

**Classification:** Injection · [CWE-89](https://cwe.mitre.org/data/definitions/89.html) · [OWASP A03:2021](https://owasp.org/Top10/A03_2021/) · walkthrough [Walkthrough §3.5](#35-sql-injection-in-product-search-routessearchts)

<a id="t-006"></a><a id="f-006"></a>
#### F-006 · Directory Listing Exposure

**Severity:** 🔴 Critical - elevated as an attack-chain keystone (individual baseline: High)  ·  **Component:** [C-01](#c-01) - Express REST API Backend  ·  **Location:** `server.ts`:270

**Issue:** The `/ftp` directory is served with full directory listing enabled. It contains confidential business documents: `acquisitions.md` (M&A plans), `incident-support.kdbx` (KeePass password database), `coupons_2013.md.bak`, and `package-lock.json.bak`. These files are publicly accessible without authentication.

**Root cause:** Confidential files, credentials, and management-plane endpoints are reachable on unauthenticated routes; SSRF lets the server fetch internal resources on the attacker's behalf; unsafe path-handling primitives leak server content.

**Evidence:** ✓ verified

```typescript
// server.ts:270
  // vuln-code-snippet start directoryListingChallenge accessLogDisclosureChallenge
  /* /ftp directory browsing and file download */ // vuln-code-snippet neutral-line directoryListingChallenge
  app.use('/ftp', serveIndexMiddleware, serveIndex('ftp', { icons: true })) // vuln-code-snippet vuln-line directoryListingChallenge
  app.use('/ftp(?!/quarantine)/:file', servePublicFiles()) // vuln-code-snippet vuln-line directoryListingChallenge
  app.use('/ftp/quarantine/:file', serveQuarantineFiles()) // vuln-code-snippet neutral-line directoryListingChallenge

  app.use('/.well-known', serveIndexMiddleware, serveIndex('.well-known', { icons: true, view: 'details' }))
```

**Fix:** ❶ [M-012](#m-012) — Confidential files exposed via /ftp directory listing server.ts

**Classification:** Unauthenticated Management Plane · [CWE-548](https://cwe.mitre.org/data/definitions/548.html) · [OWASP A01:2021](https://owasp.org/Top10/A01_2021/) · walkthrough [Walkthrough §3.6](#36-confidential-files-exposed-via-ftp-directory-listing-serve)

<a id="t-007"></a><a id="f-007"></a>
#### F-007 · Password Hash with Insufficient Effort

**Severity:** 🔴 Critical - elevated as an attack-chain keystone (individual baseline: High)  ·  **Component:** [C-01](#c-01) - Express REST API Backend  ·  **Location:** `lib/insecurity.ts`:43

**Issue:** All user passwords are hashed with unsalted `MD5`. `MD5` is cryptographically broken and password databases can be cracked entirely using rainbow tables within minutes. Any attacker who obtains a copy of the database has immediate access to all plaintext passwords.

**Root cause:** Authentication can be circumvented or forged because credentials, signing keys, or password hashes are weak, missing, or exposed.

**Evidence:** ✓ verified - A non-iterating hash is used for password storage.

**Fix:** Replace the broken hash with a salted password-hashing function (bcrypt/Argon2id) → ❶ [M-005](#m-005) — MD5 password hashing trivially reversible lib/insecurity.ts

**Classification:** Cryptographic Failures · [CWE-916](https://cwe.mitre.org/data/definitions/916.html) · [OWASP A02:2021](https://owasp.org/Top10/A02_2021/) · walkthrough [Walkthrough §3.7](#37-md5-password-hashing-trivially-reversible-libinsecurityts)

<a id="t-009"></a><a id="f-009"></a>
#### F-009 · Password Hash with Insufficient Effort

**Severity:** 🔴 Critical - elevated as an attack-chain keystone (individual baseline: High)  ·  **Component:** [C-03](#c-03) - Data Layer (SQLite + MarsDB)  ·  **Location:** `models/user.ts`:1

**Issue:** The SQLite database stores user passwords as unsalted `MD5` hashes. If an attacker gains read access to the database file (via SQL injection, path traversal, or server compromise), all password hashes can be cracked using rainbow tables instantly. Common passwords like 'admin123' would be recovered in milliseconds.

**Root cause:** Authentication can be circumvented or forged because credentials, signing keys, or password hashes are weak, missing, or exposed.

**Evidence:** ✓ verified - A non-iterating hash is used for password storage.

**Fix:** Replace the broken hash with a salted password-hashing function (bcrypt/Argon2id) → ❶ [M-005](#m-005) — MD5 password hashing trivially reversible lib/insecurity.ts

**Classification:** Cryptographic Failures · [CWE-916](https://cwe.mitre.org/data/definitions/916.html) · [OWASP A02:2021](https://owasp.org/Top10/A02_2021/) · walkthrough [Walkthrough §3.8](#38-user-passwords-stored-as-unsalted-md5-data-at-rest-modelsu)

<a id="t-010"></a><a id="f-010"></a>
#### F-010 · XML External Entity (XXE)

**Severity:** 🔴 Critical  ·  **Component:** [C-04](#c-04) - File Upload Service  ·  **Location:** `routes/fileUpload.ts`:45

**Issue:** The XML upload handler uses libxmljs2 with `noent: true`, which enables external entity processing. An attacker can upload an XML file with an XXE payload pointing to file:///etc/passwd or internal network URLs, causing the server to disclose local file contents or SSRF to internal services.

**Root cause:** User input flows into a server-side interpreter (SQL, NoSQL, XML, YAML, LDAP, OS shell) without parameterisation or schema validation.

**Evidence:** ✓ verified - An XML parser is configured to expand external entities while processing untrusted input.

```typescript
// routes/fileUpload.ts:45
                const absolutePath = path.resolve('uploads/complaints/' + fileName)
                challengeUtils.solveIf(challenges.fileWriteChallenge, () => { return absolutePath === path.resolve('ftp/legal.md') })
                if (absolutePath.includes(path.resolve('.'))) {
                  entry.pipe(fs.createWriteStream('uploads/complaints/' + fileName).on('error', function (err) { next(err) }))
                } else {
                  entry.autodrain()
                }
```

**Fix:** Disable external entity resolution on every XML parser and reject DOCTYPE declarations → ❶ [M-029](#m-029) — XXE via XML upload with external entity resolution routes/fileUpload.ts

**Classification:** Insecure File Handling · [CWE-611](https://cwe.mitre.org/data/definitions/611.html) · [OWASP A04:2021](https://owasp.org/Top10/A04_2021/) · walkthrough [Walkthrough §3.9](#39-xxe-via-xml-upload-with-external-entity-resolution-routesf)

<a id="t-011"></a><a id="f-011"></a>
#### F-011 · Code Injection

**Severity:** 🔴 Critical  ·  **Component:** [C-05](#c-05) - B2B Order API  ·  **Location:** `routes/b2bOrder.ts`:22

**Issue:** The B2B order endpoint evaluates user-supplied `orderLinesData` using `notevil` sandboxed eval inside `vm.runInContext`. The `notevil` library is not a true security sandbox - it can be escaped via prototype pollution or constructor access. An attacker can achieve full server-side code execution by sending a crafted `orderLinesData` payload.

**Root cause:** User-supplied data reaches a server-side code-execution sink (`eval`, sandbox primitives, deserialisation, prototype-pollution gadgets) and breaks out into arbitrary native execution.

**Evidence:** ✓ verified - User-supplied code is passed to a runtime evaluator without an allow-list of operations.

```typescript
// routes/b2bOrder.ts:22
      const orderLinesData = body.orderLinesData || ''
      try {
        const sandbox = { safeEval, orderLinesData }
        vm.createContext(sandbox)
        vm.runInContext('safeEval(orderLinesData)', sandbox, { timeout: 2000 })
        res.json({ cid: body.cid, orderNo: uniqueOrderNumber(), paymentDue: dateTwoWeeksFromNow() })
      } catch (err) {
```

**Fix:** Replace runtime code generation (eval/Function/template render) with a data-only execution path → ❶ [M-033](#m-033) — RCE via vm.runInContext sandbox escape in B2B order routes/b2bOrder.ts

**Classification:** Code Execution via Unsafe Deserialization or Eval · [CWE-94](https://cwe.mitre.org/data/definitions/94.html) · [OWASP A08:2021](https://owasp.org/Top10/A08_2021/) · walkthrough [Walkthrough §3.10](#310-rce-via-vmrunincontext-sandbox-escape-in-b2b-order-routes)

<a id="t-012"></a><a id="f-012"></a>
#### F-012 · Code Injection

**Severity:** 🔴 Critical  ·  **Component:** [C-01](#c-01) - Express REST API Backend  ·  **Location:** `routes/userProfile.ts`:62

**Issue:** The user profile page uses `eval()` on a server-side template that includes the user's username. If a username matches the pattern `#{(.*)}`, the captured group is passed to `eval()` in a `Node.js` context. An attacker who can set their username to `#{process.mainModule.require('child_process').execSync('cat /etc/passwd')}` achieves server-side code execution.

**Root cause:** User-supplied data reaches a server-side code-execution sink (`eval`, sandbox primitives, deserialisation, prototype-pollution gadgets) and breaks out into arbitrary native execution.

**Evidence:** ✓ verified - User-supplied code is passed to a runtime evaluator without an allow-list of operations.

```typescript
// routes/userProfile.ts:62
        if (!code) {
          throw new Error('Username is null')
        }
        username = eval(code) // eslint-disable-line no-eval
      } catch (err) {
        username = '\\' + username
      }
```

**Fix:** Replace runtime code generation (eval/Function/template render) with a data-only execution path → ❶ [M-006](#m-006) — SSTI + eval on user controlled username routes/userProfile.ts

**Classification:** Code Execution via Unsafe Deserialization or Eval · [CWE-94](https://cwe.mitre.org/data/definitions/94.html) · [OWASP A08:2021](https://owasp.org/Top10/A08_2021/) · walkthrough [Walkthrough §3.11](#311-ssti-eval-on-user-controlled-username-routesuserprofile)

<a id="t-004"></a><a id="f-004"></a>
#### F-004 · Hardcoded admin credentials static user

**Severity:** 🔴 Critical  ·  **Component:** [C-03](#c-03) - Data Layer (SQLite + MarsDB)  ·  **Location:** `data/static/users.yml`:3

**Issue:** The application seeds the database with hardcoded user credentials from `data/static/users.yml`. This includes admin@juice-sh.op:admin123, support admin credentials, and multiple customer accounts with passwords. These credentials are in the public GitHub repository and represent permanently compromised accounts.

**Evidence:** ✓ verified

```yaml
// data/static/users.yml:3
-
  email: admin
  password: '**** (8 chars)'
  key: admin
  role: 'admin'
  securityQuestion:
```

**Fix:** Move the credential out of source control into a secret store and rotate it → ❶ [M-027](#m-027) — Hardcoded admin credentials in static user data data/static/users.yml

**Classification:** Broken Authentication · [CWE-259](https://cwe.mitre.org/data/definitions/259.html) · [OWASP A07:2021](https://owasp.org/Top10/A07_2021/) · walkthrough [Walkthrough §3.4](#34-hardcoded-admin-credentials-in-static-user-data-datastatic)

### 🟠 High (24)

<a id="t-014"></a><a id="f-014"></a>
#### F-014 · Hardcoded Cryptographic Key

**Severity:** 🟠 High  ·  **Component:** [C-01](#c-01) - Express REST API Backend  ·  **Location:** `lib/insecurity.ts`:44

**Issue:** The HMAC secret used for coupon code generation is hardcoded as 'pa4qacea4VK9t9nGv7yZtwmj'. An attacker who reads the source code can generate valid coupon codes for arbitrary discounts by computing `HMAC-SHA256('pa4qacea4VK9t9nGv7yZtwmj', <data>)`.

**Root cause:** Authentication can be circumvented or forged because credentials, signing keys, or password hashes are weak, missing, or exposed.

**Evidence:** ✓ verified - A signing key is embedded as a literal constant in source.

**Fix:** Move the cryptographic key out of source control into a managed secret store and rotate it → ❷ [M-017](#m-017) — Hardcoded HMAC secret enables coupon forgery lib/insecurity.ts

**Classification:** Cryptographic Failures · [CWE-321](https://cwe.mitre.org/data/definitions/321.html) · [OWASP A02:2021](https://owasp.org/Top10/A02_2021/)

<a id="t-013"></a><a id="f-013"></a>
#### F-013 · Improper Authorization

**Severity:** 🟠 High  ·  **Component:** [C-01](#c-01) - Express REST API Backend  ·  **Location:** `server.ts`:369

**Issue:** The authorization middleware for PUT `/api/Products/:id` is intentionally commented out. Any user (including unauthenticated) can modify product names, descriptions, and prices. This is the changeProduct challenge but represents a real authorization bypass.

**Root cause:** Authorisation checks are absent or bypassable, allowing horizontal and vertical privilege jumps from a self-registered or low-rights account. Includes mass-assignment of privileged attributes.

**Evidence:** ✓ verified

```typescript
// server.ts:369
  /* Products: Only GET is allowed in order to view products */ // vuln-code-snippet neutral-line changeProductChallenge
  app.post('/api/Products', security.isAuthorized()) // vuln-code-snippet neutral-line changeProductChallenge
  // app.put('/api/Products/:id', security.isAuthorized()) // vuln-code-snippet vuln-line changeProductChallenge
  app.delete('/api/Products/:id', security.denyAll())
  /* Challenges: GET list of challenges allowed. Everything else forbidden entirely */
```

**Fix:** Add explicit server-side authorisation checks on every protected route → ❷ [M-011](#m-011) — Unauthenticated product modification via PUT /api/Products/:id server.ts

**Classification:** Broken Access Control · [CWE-285](https://cwe.mitre.org/data/definitions/285.html) · [OWASP A01:2021](https://owasp.org/Top10/A01_2021/)

<a id="t-016"></a><a id="f-016"></a>
#### F-016 · Permissive Cross-Origin (CORS) Policy

**Severity:** 🟠 High  ·  **Component:** [C-01](#c-01) - Express REST API Backend  ·  **Location:** `server.ts`:181

**Issue:** The server uses `app.use(cors())` with no origin restriction, allowing any web origin to make cross-origin requests to all API endpoints. Combined with JWT stored in localStorage (not HttpOnly cookie), this enables CSRF-style attacks from malicious websites that can read responses.

**Root cause:** A permissive CORS policy plus missing anti-CSRF tokens let any external page issue authenticated state-changing requests in the victim's session.

**Evidence:** ✓ verified

**Fix:** Replace the wildcard CORS origin with an explicit allow-list → ❷ [M-007](#m-007) — Wildcard CORS allows all origins on sensitive endpoints server.ts

**Classification:** Broken Access Control · [CWE-942](https://cwe.mitre.org/data/definitions/942.html) · [OWASP A01:2021](https://owasp.org/Top10/A01_2021/)

<a id="t-017"></a><a id="f-017"></a>
#### F-017 · NoSQL Injection

**Severity:** 🟠 High  ·  **Component:** [C-01](#c-01) - Express REST API Backend  ·  **Location:** `routes/updateProductReviews.ts`:16

**Issue:** The updateProductReviews handler accepts `req.body.id` directly as the MarsDB `_id` query without validation. Combined with `multi: true`, an attacker can update ALL reviews in the database simultaneously by passing a wildcard or operator. No author ownership check ensures only the review owner can edit.

**Root cause:** User input flows into a server-side interpreter (SQL, NoSQL, XML, YAML, LDAP, OS shell) without parameterisation or schema validation.

**Evidence:** ✓ verified

```typescript
// routes/updateProductReviews.ts:16
export function updateProductReviews () {
  return (req: Request, res: Response, next: NextFunction) => {
    const user = security.authenticatedUsers.from(req) // vuln-code-snippet vuln-line forgedReviewChallenge
    db.reviewsCollection.update( // vuln-code-snippet neutral-line forgedReviewChallenge
      { _id: req.body.id }, // vuln-code-snippet vuln-line noSqlReviewsChallenge forgedReviewChallenge
```

**Fix:** Replace string concatenation in query operators with parameter binding → ❷ [M-025](#m-025) — NoSQL injection in review update no author verification updateProductReviews.ts

**Classification:** Injection · [CWE-943](https://cwe.mitre.org/data/definitions/943.html) · [OWASP A03:2021](https://owasp.org/Top10/A03_2021/)

<a id="t-019"></a><a id="f-019"></a>
#### F-019 · Improper Authorization

**Severity:** 🟠 High  ·  **Component:** [C-01](#c-01) - Express REST API Backend  ·  **Location:** `server.ts`:362

**Issue:** GET `/api/Users` returns the full list of all registered user accounts (email, role, profile data) to any authenticated user. This enables enumeration of admin accounts and targeted attacks. The finale-rest auto-generated endpoint excludes password and totpSecret but includes all other fields.

**Root cause:** Authorisation checks are absent or bypassable, allowing horizontal and vertical privilege jumps from a self-registered or low-rights account. Includes mass-assignment of privileged attributes.

**Evidence:** ✓ verified

```typescript
// server.ts:362
  app.use('/api/Feedbacks/:id', security.isAuthorized())
  /* Users: Only POST is allowed in order to register a new user */
  app.get('/api/Users', security.isAuthorized())
  app.route('/api/Users/:id')
    .get(security.isAuthorized())
```

**Fix:** Add explicit server-side authorisation checks on every protected route → ❷ [M-010](#m-010) — User enumeration via /api/Users server.ts

**Classification:** Broken Access Control · [CWE-285](https://cwe.mitre.org/data/definitions/285.html) · [OWASP A01:2021](https://owasp.org/Top10/A01_2021/)

<a id="t-020"></a><a id="f-020"></a>
#### F-020 · Sensitive Data in Log Files

**Severity:** 🟠 High  ·  **Component:** [C-01](#c-01) - Express REST API Backend  ·  **Location:** `server.ts`:280

**Issue:** The `/support/logs` directory is served with directory listing, exposing access logs in combined Apache format. These logs reveal all user URLs visited (including order IDs, search queries, user IDs in paths), IP addresses, and user agents. This enables user activity tracking and session correlation attacks.

**Root cause:** Confidential files, credentials, and management-plane endpoints are reachable on unauthenticated routes; SSRF lets the server fetch internal resources on the attacker's behalf; unsafe path-handling primitives leak server content.

**Evidence:** ✓ verified

```typescript
// server.ts:280
  app.use('/encryptionkeys/:file', serveKeyFiles())

  /* /logs directory browsing */ // vuln-code-snippet neutral-line accessLogDisclosureChallenge
  app.use('/support/logs', serveIndexMiddleware, serveIndex('logs', { icons: true, view: 'details' })) // vuln-code-snippet vuln-line accessLogDisclosureChallenge
  app.use('/support/logs', verify.accessControlChallenges()) // vuln-code-snippet hide-line
```

**Fix:** Strip secrets and PII from every log sink and rotate any token that already leaked → ❷ [M-014](#m-014) — Access logs exposed via /support/logs directory listing server.ts

**Classification:** Missing Audit Logging & Accountability · [CWE-532](https://cwe.mitre.org/data/definitions/532.html) · [OWASP A09:2021](https://owasp.org/Top10/A09_2021/)

<a id="t-021"></a><a id="f-021"></a>
#### F-021 · Directory Listing Exposure

**Severity:** 🟠 High  ·  **Component:** [C-01](#c-01) - Express REST API Backend  ·  **Location:** `server.ts`:277

**Issue:** The `/encryptionkeys` directory is served with directory listing, exposing `jwt.pub` (the JWT public key, which paired with the hardcoded private key confirms the key pair) and `premium.key`. While the public key alone is not secret for `RS256`, this exposure combined with the hardcoded private key in source code means the complete key pair is publicly available.

**Root cause:** Confidential files, credentials, and management-plane endpoints are reachable on unauthenticated routes; SSRF lets the server fetch internal resources on the attacker's behalf; unsafe path-handling primitives leak server content.

**Evidence:** ✓ verified

```typescript
// server.ts:277

  /* /encryptionkeys directory browsing */
  app.use('/encryptionkeys', serveIndexMiddleware, serveIndex('encryptionkeys', { icons: true, view: 'details' }))
  app.use('/encryptionkeys/:file', serveKeyFiles())

```

**Fix:** ❷ [M-013](#m-013) — JWT public key and premium.key exposed at /encryptionkeys server.ts

**Classification:** Unauthenticated Management Plane · [CWE-548](https://cwe.mitre.org/data/definitions/548.html) · [OWASP A01:2021](https://owasp.org/Top10/A01_2021/)

<a id="t-024"></a><a id="f-024"></a>
#### F-024 · SQLite database file writable filesystem

**Severity:** 🟠 High _(raw Critical)_  ·  **Component:** [C-03](#c-03) - Data Layer (SQLite + MarsDB)  ·  **Location:** `models/index.ts`:36

**Issue:** The SQLite database file is stored at data/juiceshop.sqlite on the server's filesystem without encryption. Container compromise or path traversal exploits can extract the full database including all user PII, payment card numbers, TOTP secrets, and security answers.

**Root cause:** Confidential files, credentials, and management-plane endpoints are reachable on unauthenticated routes; SSRF lets the server fetch internal resources on the attacker's behalf; unsafe path-handling primitives leak server content.

**Evidence:** ✓ verified - Sensitive data is persisted or transmitted without encryption-in-transit/at-rest.

```typescript
// models/index.ts:36
    name: 'query',
    max: 5
  },
  transactionType: Transaction.TYPES.IMMEDIATE,
  storage: 'data/juiceshop.sqlite',
```

**Fix:** Encrypt the data in transit and at rest with vetted primitives → ❷ [M-028](#m-028) — SQLite database file on writable filesystem without encryption data/juiceshop.s…

**Classification:** Cryptographic Failures · [CWE-311](https://cwe.mitre.org/data/definitions/311.html) · [OWASP A02:2021](https://owasp.org/Top10/A02_2021/)

<a id="t-025"></a><a id="f-025"></a>
#### F-025 · NoSQL Injection

**Severity:** 🟠 High  ·  **Component:** [C-01](#c-01) - Express REST API Backend  ·  **Location:** `routes/showProductReviews.ts`:35

**Issue:** The showProductReviews handler passes user-supplied product ID directly into a MarsDB `$where` JavaScript evaluation clause. An attacker can inject JavaScript conditions to extract all reviews across all products or trigger sleep-based denial-of-service. The ID is only truncated to 40 chars when the challenge is enabled.

**Root cause:** User input flows into a server-side interpreter (SQL, NoSQL, XML, YAML, LDAP, OS shell) without parameterisation or schema validation.

**Evidence:** ✓ verified

```typescript
// routes/showProductReviews.ts:35
    // Measure how long the query takes, to check if there was a nosql dos attack
    const t0 = new Date().getTime()

    db.reviewsCollection.find({ $where: 'this.product == ' + id }).then((reviews: Review[]) => {
      const t1 = new Date().getTime()
```

**Fix:** Replace string concatenation in query operators with parameter binding → ❷ [M-024](#m-024) — NoSQL injection via \$where clause in product reviews showProductReviews.ts

**Classification:** Injection · [CWE-943](https://cwe.mitre.org/data/definitions/943.html) · [OWASP A03:2021](https://owasp.org/Top10/A03_2021/)

<a id="t-026"></a><a id="f-026"></a>
#### F-026 · Server-Side Request Forgery (SSRF)

**Severity:** 🟠 High  ·  **Component:** [C-04](#c-04) - File Upload Service  ·  **Location:** `routes/profileImageUrlUpload.ts`:1

**Issue:** The profile image URL upload endpoint fetches images from user-supplied URLs server-side without validation of the URL scheme or host. An attacker can supply internal URLs (file://, http://169.254.169.254/metadata, http://localhost:3000/metrics) to probe internal services or read local files via the SSRF vector.

**Root cause:** Confidential files, credentials, and management-plane endpoints are reachable on unauthenticated routes; SSRF lets the server fetch internal resources on the attacker's behalf; unsafe path-handling primitives leak server content.

**Evidence:** ✓ verified - An outbound request is issued to a URL derived from untrusted input without an allow-list.

```typescript
// routes/profileImageUrlUpload.ts:1
/*
 * Copyright (c) 2014-2026 Bjoern Kimminich & the OWASP Juice Shop contributors.
 * SPDX-License-Identifier: MIT
```

**Fix:** Validate the URL scheme + host against an explicit allow-list before issuing outbound requests → ❷ [M-031](#m-031) — SSRF via profile image URL upload routes/profileImageUrlUpload.ts

**Classification:** Server-Side Request Forgery · [CWE-918](https://cwe.mitre.org/data/definitions/918.html) · [OWASP A10:2021](https://owasp.org/Top10/A10_2021/)

<a id="t-027"></a><a id="f-027"></a>
#### F-027 · OAuth token exposed URL fragment

**Severity:** 🟠 High  ·  **Component:** [C-02](#c-02) - Angular Single-Page Application  ·  **Location:** `frontend/src/app/app.routing.ts`:237

**Issue:** The OAuth component processes the access token from the URL fragment (`window.location.href`). URL fragments containing tokens can be leaked via Referer headers, browser history, and server logs. This is the implicit OAuth flow which is deprecated due to token leakage risks.

**Evidence:** ✓ verified

```typescript
// frontend/src/app/app.routing.ts:237
  {
    matcher: oauthMatcher,
    data: { params: (window.location.href).substr(window.location.href.indexOf('#')) },
    component: OAuthComponent
  },
```

**Fix:** ❷ [M-023](#m-023) — OAuth token exposed in URL fragment oauth/oauth.component.ts

**Classification:** Cryptographic Failures · [CWE-598](https://cwe.mitre.org/data/definitions/598.html) · [OWASP A02:2021](https://owasp.org/Top10/A02_2021/)

<a id="t-028"></a><a id="f-028"></a>
#### F-028 · Insecure Storage of Sensitive Information

**Severity:** 🟠 High  ·  **Component:** [C-02](#c-02) - Angular Single-Page Application  ·  **Location:** `frontend/src/app/login/login.component.ts`:101

**Issue:** The JWT authentication token is stored in localStorage, which is accessible to any JavaScript running on the page. A successful XSS attack can exfiltrate the token via `localStorage.getItem('token')`, allowing session hijacking. HttpOnly cookies would prevent JavaScript access.

**Root cause:** Attacker-controlled content is rendered in the victim's browser without sanitisation; combined with session tokens held in JavaScript-readable storage, any payload yields immediate account takeover.

**Evidence:** ✓ verified

**Fix:** ❷ [M-020](#m-020) — JWT stored in localStorage XSS accessible token storage login.component.ts

**Classification:** Insecure Client-Side Storage · [CWE-922](https://cwe.mitre.org/data/definitions/922.html) · [OWASP A02:2021](https://owasp.org/Top10/A02_2021/)

<a id="t-029"></a><a id="f-029"></a>
#### F-029 · Uncontrolled Resource Consumption

**Severity:** 🟠 High  ·  **Component:** [C-05](#c-05) - B2B Order API  ·  **Location:** `routes/b2bOrder.ts`:30

**Issue:** An attacker can craft an `orderLinesData` payload that causes an infinite loop inside `notevil`'s evaluator. While `notevil` detects this and throws, the 2000ms execution timeout in `vm.runInContext` will block the `Node.js` event loop for 2 seconds per request, enabling a simple amplification DoS attack.

**Evidence:** ✓ verified

**Fix:** Bound the request rate and the per-request resource budget on this endpoint → ❷ [M-034](#m-034) — Denial of Service via B2B infinite loop routes/b2bOrder.ts

**Classification:** Denial of Service · [CWE-400](https://cwe.mitre.org/data/definitions/400.html) · [OWASP A04:2021](https://owasp.org/Top10/A04_2021/)

<a id="t-030"></a><a id="f-030"></a>
#### F-030 · Missing Rate Limiting (Brute-Force)

**Severity:** 🟠 High  ·  **Component:** [C-01](#c-01) - Express REST API Backend  ·  **Location:** `server.ts`:343

**Issue:** The login endpoint at POST `/rest/user/login` has no rate limiting. An attacker can perform unlimited brute-force credential attacks. Combined with the known user emails from `/api/Users` and hardcoded credentials in `users.yml`, account compromise is trivial.

**Evidence:** ✓ verified

```typescript
// server.ts:343
  /* Rate limiting */
  app.enable('trust proxy')
  app.use('/rest/user/reset-password', rateLimit({
    windowMs: 5 * 60 * 1000,
    max: 100,
```

**Fix:** Apply rate limiting and lock-out thresholds on authentication endpoints → ❷ [M-008](#m-008) — No rate limiting on login endpoint server.ts

**Classification:** Broken Authentication · [CWE-307](https://cwe.mitre.org/data/definitions/307.html) · [OWASP A07:2021](https://owasp.org/Top10/A07_2021/)

<a id="t-031"></a><a id="f-031"></a>
#### F-031 · Denial Service XML billion laughs

**Severity:** 🟠 High  ·  **Component:** [C-04](#c-04) - File Upload Service  ·  **Location:** `routes/fileUpload.ts`:45

**Issue:** The XML parser with `noent: true` is vulnerable to billion-laughs (XML DoS) attacks where entity expansion causes exponential memory allocation. While `vm.runInContext` has a 2000ms timeout, memory exhaustion can still crash the `Node.js` process before the timeout triggers.

**Evidence:** ✓ verified

```typescript
// routes/fileUpload.ts:45
                challengeUtils.solveIf(challenges.fileWriteChallenge, () => { return absolutePath === path.resolve('ftp/legal.md') })
                if (absolutePath.includes(path.resolve('.'))) {
                  entry.pipe(fs.createWriteStream('uploads/complaints/' + fileName).on('error', function (err) { next(err) }))
                } else {
                  entry.autodrain()
```

**Fix:** ❷ [M-032](#m-032) — Denial of Service via XML billion laughs attack routes/fileUpload.ts

**Classification:** Denial of Service · [CWE-776](https://cwe.mitre.org/data/definitions/776.html) · [OWASP A04:2021](https://owasp.org/Top10/A04_2021/)

<a id="t-032"></a><a id="f-032"></a>
#### F-032 · GitHub Actions not using least

**Severity:** 🟠 High  ·  **Component:** [C-05](#c-05) - B2B Order API  ·  **Location:** `.github/workflows/ci.yml`:1

**Issue:** The CI/CD workflow does not specify a top-level `permissions:` block, defaulting to potentially broad read access to the repository. Some workflow jobs deploy to Docker Hub, Heroku, and GitHub Packages, meaning a compromise of the CI workflow (via unpinned Action substitution) could exfiltrate all repository secrets.

**Evidence:** ✓ verified

```yaml
// .github/workflows/ci.yml:1
name: "CI/CD Pipeline"
on:
  push:
```

**Fix:** ❷ [M-038](#m-038) — GitHub Actions not using least privilege permissions .github/workflows/ci.yml

**Classification:** Broken Access Control · [CWE-272](https://cwe.mitre.org/data/definitions/272.html) · [OWASP A01:2021](https://owasp.org/Top10/A01_2021/)

<a id="t-033"></a><a id="f-033"></a>
#### F-033 · Improper Authorization

**Severity:** 🟠 High  ·  **Component:** [C-01](#c-01) - Express REST API Backend  ·  **Location:** `server.ts`:423

**Issue:** The B2B order endpoint at `/b2b/v2/orders` is protected only by `security.isAuthorized()` - any authenticated user (including regular customers) can access it. The B2B API should only be accessible to specific B2B customer accounts with appropriate roles.

**Root cause:** Authorisation checks are absent or bypassable, allowing horizontal and vertical privilege jumps from a self-registered or low-rights account. Includes mass-assignment of privileged attributes.

**Evidence:** ✓ verified

```typescript
// server.ts:423
  app.post('/api/Users', verify.emptyUserRegistration())
  /* Unauthorized users are not allowed to access B2B API */
  app.use('/b2b/v2', security.isAuthorized())
  /* Check if the quantity is available in stock and limit per user not exceeded, then add item to basket */
  app.put('/api/BasketItems/:id', security.appendUserId(), basketItems.quantityCheckBeforeBasketItemUpdate())
```

**Fix:** Add explicit server-side authorisation checks on every protected route → ❷ [M-035](#m-035) — B2B API reachable by any authenticated user server.ts

**Classification:** Broken Access Control · [CWE-285](https://cwe.mitre.org/data/definitions/285.html) · [OWASP A01:2021](https://owasp.org/Top10/A01_2021/)

<a id="t-034"></a><a id="f-034"></a>
#### F-034 · Use of Unmaintained Third-Party Components

**Severity:** 🟠 High  ·  **Component:** [C-05](#c-05) - B2B Order API  ·  **Location:** `.dependabot/config.yml`:9

**Issue:** Three known-vulnerable packages are explicitly excluded from Dependabot auto-updates: `express-jwt@0.1.3` (JWT `alg:none` bypass), `sanitize-html@1.4.2` (XSS bypass), and `unzipper@0.9.15` (path traversal). While intentional for training, this creates a permanent CI/CD supply chain risk where newly discovered exploits in these pinned packages can be weaponized without any automated detection.

**Evidence:** ✓ verified

```yaml
// .dependabot/config.yml:9
    default_reviewers:
      - "bkimminich"
    default_labels:
      - "dependencies"
    ignored_updates:
```

**Fix:** Replace the unmaintained dependency with a maintained equivalent or fork it under ownership → ❷ [M-037](#m-037) — Dependabot vulnerability pinning suppresses security updates .dependabot/config…

**Classification:** Supply-Chain Integrity · [CWE-1104](https://cwe.mitre.org/data/definitions/1104.html) · [OWASP A06:2021](https://owasp.org/Top10/A06_2021/)

<a id="t-035"></a><a id="f-035"></a>
#### F-035 · Missing Defense-in-Depth Control

**Severity:** 🟠 High  ·  **Component:** [C-01](#c-01) - Express REST API Backend  ·  **Location:** `server.ts`:185

**Issue:** No Content-Security-Policy header is configured. The `helmet.xssFilter()` middleware is explicitly commented out. Without CSP, stored XSS attacks can execute arbitrary JavaScript, load external scripts, and exfiltrate data.

**Evidence:** ✓ verified - The control that would block this exposure is absent from the configured stack.

**Fix:** Add the missing protection mechanism for this surface (CSP / CSRF token / headers) → ❷ [M-016](#m-016) — Missing Content Security Policy allows unrestricted script execution server.ts

**Classification:** Denial of Service · [CWE-693](https://cwe.mitre.org/data/definitions/693.html) · [OWASP A04:2021](https://owasp.org/Top10/A04_2021/)

<a id="t-036"></a><a id="f-036"></a>
#### F-036 · Path Traversal

**Severity:** 🟠 High  ·  **Component:** [C-04](#c-04) - File Upload Service  ·  **Location:** `routes/fileUpload.ts`:44

**Issue:** The ZIP file upload handler extracts entries to uploads/complaints/ without sufficient path validation. The `absolutePath.includes(path.resolve('.'))` check can be bypassed with carefully crafted paths. An attacker can write files to arbitrary locations including ftp/ (e.g. `ftp/legal.md`), enabling overwrite of sensitive server files.

**Root cause:** Confidential files, credentials, and management-plane endpoints are reachable on unauthenticated routes; SSRF lets the server fetch internal resources on the attacker's behalf; unsafe path-handling primitives leak server content.

**Evidence:** ✓ verified - User-supplied path components are joined without traversal-safe canonicalisation.

```typescript
// routes/fileUpload.ts:44
                const absolutePath = path.resolve('uploads/complaints/' + fileName)
                challengeUtils.solveIf(challenges.fileWriteChallenge, () => { return absolutePath === path.resolve('ftp/legal.md') })
                if (absolutePath.includes(path.resolve('.'))) {
                  entry.pipe(fs.createWriteStream('uploads/complaints/' + fileName).on('error', function (err) { next(err) }))
                } else {
```

**Fix:** Resolve and normalise every constructed path and reject anything that escapes the intended base directory → ❷ [M-030](#m-030) — Directory traversal in ZIP file upload routes/fileUpload.ts

**Classification:** Insecure File Handling · [CWE-22](https://cwe.mitre.org/data/definitions/22.html) · [OWASP A04:2021](https://owasp.org/Top10/A04_2021/)

<a id="t-038"></a><a id="f-038"></a>
#### F-038 · Client side only admin guard

**Severity:** 🟠 High  ·  **Component:** [C-02](#c-02) - Angular Single-Page Application  ·  **Location:** `frontend/src/app/app.guard.ts`:18

**Issue:** AdminGuard and AccountingGuard are client-side only Angular route guards. They can be bypassed by directly navigating to `/administration` or `/accounting` with a non-admin JWT. The backend has no matching server-side authorization check that restricts the admin UI's data endpoints to admin roles.

**Evidence:** ✓ verified

**Fix:** ❷ [M-021](#m-021) — Client side only admin guard Angular route guard bypass app.guard.ts

**Classification:** Broken Access Control · [CWE-602](https://cwe.mitre.org/data/definitions/602.html) · [OWASP A01:2021](https://owasp.org/Top10/A01_2021/)

<a id="t-015"></a><a id="f-015"></a>
#### F-015 · Cross-Site Request Forgery (CSRF)

**Severity:** 🟠 High  ·  **Component:** [C-01](#c-01) - Express REST API Backend  ·  **Location:** `server.ts`:289

**Issue:** No CSRF token or SameSite cookie protection is implemented. The cookie-parser middleware uses a weak secret ('kekse'). While JWT in Authorization headers provides some CSRF resistance, endpoints using cookie-based auth (profile page uses `req.cookies.token`) are vulnerable to CSRF from any origin due to wildcard CORS.

**Root cause:** A permissive CORS policy plus missing anti-CSRF tokens let any external page issue authenticated state-changing requests in the victim's session.

**Evidence:** ✓ verified - A state-changing request can be triggered cross-origin without a synchroniser token.

**Fix:** Enforce a same-origin or signed CSRF token on every state-changing endpoint → ❷ [M-009](#m-009) — No CSRF protection on state changing endpoints server.ts

**Classification:** Cross-Site Request Forgery (CSRF) · [CWE-352](https://cwe.mitre.org/data/definitions/352.html) · [OWASP A01:2021](https://owasp.org/Top10/A01_2021/)

<a id="t-018"></a><a id="f-018"></a>
#### F-018 · Cross-Site Scripting

**Severity:** 🟠 High  ·  **Component:** [C-02](#c-02) - Angular Single-Page Application  ·  **Location:** `frontend/src/app/search-result/search-result.component.spec.ts`:88

**Issue:** Search results are rendered using bypassSecurityTrustHtml, allowing stored XSS payloads injected via product names/descriptions (which are updatable by any authenticated user due to PUT `/api/Products/:id` being unprotected) to execute in the browser of any user who views search results.

**Root cause:** Attacker-controlled content is rendered in the victim's browser without sanitisation; combined with session tokens held in JavaScript-readable storage, any payload yields immediate account takeover.

**Evidence:** ✓ verified - User input is rendered as HTML without contextual output encoding.

```typescript
// frontend/src/app/search-result/search-result.component.spec.ts:88
    translateService.onDefaultLangChange = new EventEmitter()
    sanitizer = jasmine.createSpyObj('DomSanitizer', ['bypassSecurityTrustHtml', 'sanitize'])
    sanitizer.bypassSecurityTrustHtml.and.returnValue(of({}))
    sanitizer.sanitize.and.returnValue({})
    activatedRoute = new MockActivatedRoute()
```

**Fix:** Output-encode untrusted strings at every sink and remove all `bypassSecurityTrustHtml` calls → ❷ [M-022](#m-022) — DOM XSS via search result rendering search result.component.ts

**Classification:** Cross-Site Scripting (XSS) · [CWE-79](https://cwe.mitre.org/data/definitions/79.html) · [OWASP A03:2021](https://owasp.org/Top10/A03_2021/)

<a id="t-037"></a><a id="f-037"></a>
#### F-037 · Cross-Site Scripting

**Severity:** 🟠 High  ·  **Component:** [C-02](#c-02) - Angular Single-Page Application  ·  **Location:** `frontend/src/app/administration/administration.component.ts`:60

**Issue:** The administration component renders user emails and feedback comments using `DomSanitizer.bypassSecurityTrustHtml()`, which disables Angular's built-in XSS protection. An attacker who registers with an email containing a script tag or injects malicious HTML via feedback can execute arbitrary JavaScript in the browser of any admin who views the administration panel.

**Root cause:** Attacker-controlled content is rendered in the victim's browser without sanitisation; combined with session tokens held in JavaScript-readable storage, any payload yields immediate account takeover.

**Evidence:** ✓ verified - User input is rendered as HTML without contextual output encoding.

```typescript
// frontend/src/app/administration/administration.component.ts:60
        this.userDataSourceHidden = users
        for (const user of this.userDataSource) {
          user.email = this.sanitizer.bypassSecurityTrustHtml(`<span class="${this.doesUserHaveAnActiveSession(user) ? 'confirmation' : 'error'}">${user.email}</span>`)
        }
        this.userDataSource = new MatTableDataSource(this.userDataSource)
```

**Fix:** Output-encode untrusted strings at every sink and remove all `bypassSecurityTrustHtml` calls → ❷ [M-019](#m-019) — Stored XSS via trust HTML bypass in admin panel administration.component.ts

**Classification:** Cross-Site Scripting (XSS) · [CWE-79](https://cwe.mitre.org/data/definitions/79.html) · [OWASP A03:2021](https://owasp.org/Top10/A03_2021/)

### 🟡 Medium (3)

<a id="t-040"></a><a id="f-040"></a>
#### F-040 · Insufficient Logging

**Severity:** 🟡 Medium  ·  **Component:** [C-05](#c-05) - B2B Order API  ·  **Location:** `routes/b2bOrder.ts`:18

**Issue:** B2B orders processed through the `/b2b/v2/orders` endpoint are not logged with sufficient detail to reconstruct what orderLinesData was submitted. If an attacker abuses the RCE vulnerability, there is no forensic record of the malicious payload that was executed.

**Evidence:** ✓ verified

**Fix:** ❸ [M-036](#m-036) — No audit trail for B2B orders routes/b2bOrder.ts

**Classification:** Missing Audit Logging & Accountability · [CWE-778](https://cwe.mitre.org/data/definitions/778.html) · [OWASP A09:2021](https://owasp.org/Top10/A09_2021/)

<a id="t-041"></a><a id="f-041"></a>
#### F-041 · Information Disclosure

**Severity:** 🟡 Medium  ·  **Component:** [C-01](#c-01) - Express REST API Backend  ·  **Location:** `server.ts`:718

**Issue:** The Prometheus metrics endpoint is publicly accessible without authentication. It exposes application internals including request counts, durations, error rates, file upload metrics, and startup timing - information that aids attackers in fingerprinting and targeting attacks.

**Root cause:** Confidential files, credentials, and management-plane endpoints are reachable on unauthenticated routes; SSRF lets the server fetch internal resources on the attacker's behalf; unsafe path-handling primitives leak server content.

**Evidence:** ✓ verified

**Fix:** Restrict the response to the minimum fields needed and never echo secrets → ❸ [M-015](#m-015) — Prometheus metrics exposed unauthenticated at /metrics server.ts

**Classification:** Error Information Disclosure · [CWE-200](https://cwe.mitre.org/data/definitions/200.html) · [OWASP A05:2021](https://owasp.org/Top10/A05_2021/)

<a id="t-039"></a><a id="f-039"></a>
#### F-039 · Open Redirect

**Severity:** 🟡 Medium  ·  **Component:** [C-01](#c-01) - Express REST API Backend  ·  **Location:** `routes/redirect.ts`:19

**Issue:** The redirect endpoint validates the target URL against an allowlist, but the validation can be bypassed through subdomain matching or URL manipulation. An attacker can craft phishing links using the trusted Juice Shop domain that redirect to malicious sites.

**Root cause:** Confidential files, credentials, and management-plane endpoints are reachable on unauthenticated routes; SSRF lets the server fetch internal resources on the attacker's behalf; unsafe path-handling primitives leak server content.

**Evidence:** ✓ verified

**Fix:** ❸ [M-018](#m-018) — Open redirect via /redirect?to= parameter routes/redirect.ts

**Classification:** Open Redirect · [CWE-601](https://cwe.mitre.org/data/definitions/601.html) · [OWASP A01:2021](https://owasp.org/Top10/A01_2021/)

---

_**Severity annotation:** rows tagged `*(raw Critical)*` had a Critical-class impact that was capped to a lower effective severity by the triage stage (likelihood downgrade or `data/severity-caps.yaml` rule). The rendered severity is the **effective** severity used for ranking and prioritisation; the raw severity is preserved here so reviewers can re-evaluate the cap decision._

## 9. Abuse Cases

_Abuse cases describe end-to-end attack scenarios that chain individual findings into an exploitation path. Each case is **mandatory** - defined in the org profile / plugin library and evaluated against every repository. Every chain step references a finding from [§8 Findings Register](#8-findings-register); each step is code-confirmed against the repository and the chain verdict is folded deterministically from the per-step results, never rated by hand._

| # | Scenario | Actor | Combined Risk | Verdict |
|--------|------------------------------------|------------------|-------------|--------------|
| [AC-T-001](#ac-t-001) | Account Takeover via Stored XSS + Token Hijacking | external-attacker | 🟠 High | ◐ Partially blocked |
| [AC-T-002](#ac-t-002) | Bulk Data Exfiltration via Broken Object Authorization | authenticated-user | 🟠 High | ◐ Partially blocked |
| [AC-T-003](#ac-t-003) | Privilege Escalation to Admin via JWT Algorithm Confusion | external-attacker | 🔴 Critical | ⚠ Fully viable |
| [AC-T-005](#ac-t-005) | Authentication Bypass via Exposed Secret Material | external-attacker | 🔴 Critical | ⚠ Fully viable |
| [AC-T-006](#ac-t-006) | Remote Code Execution via Server-Side Injection | external-attacker | 🔴 Critical | ◐ Partially blocked |

_Verdict: ⚠ Fully viable - no effective control blocks this chain · ◐ Partially blocked - at least one step has a compensating control but the chain is not fully closed · ✓ Mitigated - chain is broken at a verified step · ? Inconclusive - could not be verified end-to-end._

---

### <a id="ac-t-001"></a>AC-T-001 — Account Takeover via Stored XSS + Token Hijacking

> **Source:** mandatory · **Actor:** external-attacker - unauthenticated external attacker · **Combined Risk:** 🟠 High · **Verdict:** ◐ Partially blocked

**Goal:** Obtain persistent authenticated access as an arbitrary user without valid credentials.

**Prerequisite:** Attacker can submit content that is later rendered to other users (e.g. feedback, comments, profile fields).

**Attack chain**

| Step | Finding | Outcome |
|--------|------------------------------------------------|----------------------|
| 1 | [F-018](#f-018) — DOM XSS via search result rendering search result.component.ts<br/>`frontend/src/app/search-result/search-result.component.ts:132` | Attacker JavaScript executes in the victim's browser session. |
| 2 | [F-028](#f-028) — JWT stored in localStorage XSS accessible token storage login.component.ts | Token exfiltrated from local/session storage via the Step 1 payload. |
| 3 | [F-027](#f-027) — OAuth token exposed in URL fragment oauth/oauth.component.ts<br/>`lib/insecurity.ts:54` | Exfiltrated token accepted for a new session; absence of token binding / PKCE removes the last server-side revocation opportunity. |

**Why combined risk exceeds individual ratings**

Individually the XSS sink and the web-readable token storage rate below Critical, but chained they form a repeatable credential-theft path: a single stored payload causes indefinite session compromise for every user who views the affected page.

**Blocking mitigations**

Implementing any single mitigation below severs the chain at the named step, so the end-to-end abuse can no longer complete:

- [M-022](#m-022) — DOM XSS via search result rendering search result.component.ts (**P2**): remediating [F-018](#f-018) — DOM XSS via search result rendering search result.component.ts breaks the chain at **Step 1**, removing the link the rest of the chain depends on.
- [M-020](#m-020) — JWT stored in localStorage XSS accessible token storage login.component.ts (**P2**): remediating [F-028](#f-028) — JWT stored in localStorage XSS accessible token storage login.component.ts breaks the chain at **Step 2**, removing the link the rest of the chain depends on.
- [M-023](#m-023) — OAuth token exposed in URL fragment oauth/oauth.component.ts (**P2**): remediating [F-027](#f-027) — OAuth token exposed in URL fragment oauth/oauth.component.ts breaks the chain at **Step 3**, removing the link the rest of the chain depends on.

---

### <a id="ac-t-002"></a>AC-T-002 — Bulk Data Exfiltration via Broken Object Authorization

> **Source:** mandatory · **Actor:** authenticated-user - authenticated low-privilege user · **Combined Risk:** 🟠 High · **Verdict:** ◐ Partially blocked

**Goal:** Enumerate and exfiltrate other users' records, then escalate own permissions via unguarded mass assignment.

**Prerequisite:** Attacker holds a valid, non-privileged user account.

**Attack chain**

| Step | Finding | Outcome |
|--------|------------------------------------------------|----------------------|
| 1 | [F-017](#f-017) — NoSQL injection in review update no author verification updateProductReviews.ts<br/>`routes/basket.ts:19` | Attacker enumerates and retrieves records for arbitrary object IDs; no ownership comparison is performed. |
| 2 | _no matching finding_<br/>`routes/verify.ts:52` | Update endpoint persists an unfiltered `role` (or equivalent) field supplied in the request body. |

**Why combined risk exceeds individual ratings**

The ownership gap exposes every record, and the mass-assignment gap lets the same low-privilege actor self-elevate - together they turn a single compromised account into full tenant data access and role escalation.

**Blocking mitigations**

Implementing any single mitigation below severs the chain at the named step, so the end-to-end abuse can no longer complete:

- [M-025](#m-025) — NoSQL injection in review update no author verification updateProductReviews.ts (**P2**): remediating [F-017](#f-017) — NoSQL injection in review update no author verification updateProductReviews.ts breaks the chain at **Step 1**, removing the link the rest of the chain depends on.

---

### <a id="ac-t-003"></a>AC-T-003 — Privilege Escalation to Admin via JWT Algorithm Confusion

> **Source:** mandatory · **Actor:** external-attacker - unauthenticated external attacker · **Combined Risk:** 🔴 Critical · **Verdict:** ⚠ Fully viable

**Goal:** Forge an admin-role JWT without knowledge of the signing secret.

**Prerequisite:** Attacker can obtain any valid JWT issued by the system (e.g. by registering a free account).

**Attack chain**

| Step | Finding | Outcome |
|--------|------------------------------------------------|----------------------|
| 1 | [F-002](#f-002) — Hardcoded RSA private key enables JWT forgery lib/insecurity.ts | Verifier accepts attacker-chosen `alg` (e.g. `none` or HMAC-with-public-key), allowing token re-signing without the secret. |
| 2 | [F-038](#f-038) — Client side only admin guard Angular route guard bypass app.guard.ts<br/>`lib/insecurity.ts:159` | Forged `role: admin` claim is accepted as authoritative because the role is not re-fetched from the database per request. |

**Why combined risk exceeds individual ratings**

Algorithm confusion alone yields a forgeable token; trusting the in-token role claim turns that forgery into instant admin access - neither gap is Critical in isolation, but the chain is a full authentication bypass.

**Blocking mitigations**

Implementing any single mitigation below severs the chain at the named step, so the end-to-end abuse can no longer complete:

- [M-003](#m-003) — Hardcoded RSA private key enables JWT forgery lib/insecurity.ts (**P1**): remediating [F-002](#f-002) — Hardcoded RSA private key enables JWT forgery lib/insecurity.ts breaks the chain at **Step 1**, removing the link the rest of the chain depends on.
- [M-021](#m-021) — Client side only admin guard Angular route guard bypass app.guard.ts (**P2**): remediating [F-038](#f-038) — Client side only admin guard Angular route guard bypass app.guard.ts breaks the chain at **Step 2**, removing the link the rest of the chain depends on.

---

### <a id="ac-t-005"></a>AC-T-005 — Authentication Bypass via Exposed Secret Material

> **Source:** mandatory · **Actor:** external-attacker - unauthenticated external attacker · **Combined Risk:** 🔴 Critical · **Verdict:** ⚠ Fully viable

**Goal:** Forge trusted tokens / credentials and impersonate any user.

**Prerequisite:** Signing material or other secrets are reachable (committed to a public repo, served by an unauthenticated route, or in an exposed directory).

**Attack chain**

| Step | Finding | Outcome |
|--------|------------------------------------------------|----------------------|
| 1 | [F-002](#f-002) — Hardcoded RSA private key enables JWT forgery lib/insecurity.ts | A private key, signing secret, or credential file is committed to the source repository or served without authentication. |
| 2 | [F-002](#f-002) — Hardcoded RSA private key enables JWT forgery lib/insecurity.ts | The exposed key/secret is the same one the server trusts, so a token signed with it (or the leaked credential) is accepted as authentic. |

**Why combined risk exceeds individual ratings**

Exposed signing material collapses the entire authentication boundary: any attacker who reads the key can mint a valid token for any identity or role, with no credential ever required.

**Blocking mitigations**

Implementing any single mitigation below severs the chain at the named step, so the end-to-end abuse can no longer complete:

- [M-003](#m-003) — Hardcoded RSA private key enables JWT forgery lib/insecurity.ts (**P1**): remediating [F-002](#f-002) — Hardcoded RSA private key enables JWT forgery lib/insecurity.ts breaks the chain at **Step 1**, removing the link the rest of the chain depends on.

---

### <a id="ac-t-006"></a>AC-T-006 — Remote Code Execution via Server-Side Injection

> **Source:** mandatory · **Actor:** external-attacker - unauthenticated external attacker · **Combined Risk:** 🔴 Critical · **Verdict:** ◐ Partially blocked

**Goal:** Execute arbitrary code in the application process.

**Prerequisite:** An input reaches a server-side interpreter / template / eval.

**Attack chain**

| Step | Finding | Outcome |
|--------|------------------------------------------------|----------------------|
| 1 | [F-011](#f-011) — RCE via vm.runInContext sandbox escape in B2B order routes/b2bOrder.ts<br/>`routes/userProfile.ts:62` | Attacker-controlled input is passed to `eval`, a server-side template engine, an unsafe sandbox, or an unsafe deserializer. |

**Why combined risk exceeds individual ratings**

A single injection into a server-side interpreter yields code execution in the application process - the highest-impact outcome, granting full filesystem and network access from one unauthenticated request.

**Blocking mitigations**

Implementing any single mitigation below severs the chain at the named step, so the end-to-end abuse can no longer complete:

- [M-033](#m-033) — RCE via vm.runInContext sandbox escape in B2B order routes/b2bOrder.ts (**P1**): remediating [F-011](#f-011) — RCE via vm.runInContext sandbox escape in B2B order routes/b2bOrder.ts breaks the chain at **Step 1**, removing the link the rest of the chain depends on.

### P1 — Immediate

<a id="m-001"></a>
#### M-001 — SQL injection in login routes/login.ts

**Addresses:**

- 🔴 [F-001](#f-001) — SQL injection in login

**Priority:** P1 - Immediate · **Effort:** Medium · **File:** `routes/login.ts:37`

```typescript
// Reject string interpolation; use parameter binding.
await sequelize.query(
  'SELECT * FROM Users WHERE email = :email AND password = :password',
  { replacements: { email, password: hash(password) }, type: QueryTypes.SELECT }
)
```

**Verification:** Submit `' OR 1=1 --` in the affected field and confirm the request returns 401 (or zero rows) instead of an authenticated session.

---

<a id="m-002"></a>
#### M-002 — SQL injection in product search routes/search.ts

**Addresses:**

- 🔴 [F-005](#f-005) — SQL injection in product search

**Priority:** P1 - Immediate · **Effort:** Medium · **File:** `routes/search.ts:23`

```typescript
// Reject string interpolation; use parameter binding.
await sequelize.query(
  'SELECT * FROM Users WHERE email = :email AND password = :password',
  { replacements: { email, password: hash(password) }, type: QueryTypes.SELECT }
)
```

**Verification:** Submit `' OR 1=1 --` in the affected field and confirm the request returns 401 (or zero rows) instead of an authenticated session.

---

<a id="m-003"></a>
#### M-003 — Hardcoded RSA private key enables JWT forgery lib/insecurity.ts

**Addresses:**

- 🔴 [F-002](#f-002) — Hardcoded RSA private key enables JWT forgery

**Priority:** P1 - Immediate · **Effort:** Medium · **File:** `lib/insecurity.ts:26`

```typescript
// Load the RSA private key from an environment variable / KMS — never
// the source tree. Rotate the prior key and revoke outstanding tokens.
const privateKey = process.env.JWT_PRIVATE_KEY
if (!privateKey) throw new Error('JWT_PRIVATE_KEY not set')
const token = jwt.sign(claims, privateKey, { algorithm: 'RS256' })
```

**Verification:** `git grep -- 'BEGIN RSA PRIVATE KEY'` returns no matches in the working tree.

---

<a id="m-004"></a>
#### M-004 — JWT algorithm none bypass via express jwt lib/insecurity.ts

**Addresses:**

- 🔴 [F-003](#f-003) — JWT algorithm none bypass via express jwt lib/insecurity.ts

**Priority:** P1 - Immediate · **Effort:** Medium · **File:** `package.json:1`

```typescript
// jsonwebtoken@9+: pin algorithm whitelist; reject `alg:none`.
jwt.verify(token, publicKey, { algorithms: ['RS256'] })
```

**Verification:** Submit a token with `alg:none` (no signature) and confirm `jwt.verify()` throws instead of returning the decoded claims.

---

<a id="m-005"></a>
#### M-005 — MD5 password hashing trivially reversible lib/insecurity.ts

**Addresses:**

- 🔴 [F-007](#f-007) — MD5 password hashing trivially reversible
- 🔴 [F-009](#f-009) — User passwords stored as unsalted MD5 data at rest

**Priority:** P1 - Immediate · **Effort:** Medium · **File:** `lib/insecurity.ts:43`

```typescript
// Replace md5/sha1 password hashes with bcrypt or argon2.
import bcrypt from 'bcrypt'
const hash = await bcrypt.hash(plaintext, 12)
const ok = await bcrypt.compare(plaintext, storedHash)
```

**Verification:** `select password from Users limit 1;` returns a `$2b$12$...` bcrypt prefix, not a 32-hex md5 string.

---

<a id="m-006"></a>
#### M-006 — SSTI + eval on user controlled username routes/userProfile.ts

**Addresses:**

- 🔴 [F-012](#f-012) — SSTI + eval on user controlled username

**Priority:** P1 - Immediate · **Effort:** Medium · **File:** `routes/userProfile.ts:62`

```javascript
// Replace `eval()` / sandbox with a strict whitelist parser.
import { evaluate } from 'mathjs'
const allowed = /^[\d+\-*/().\s]+$/
if (!allowed.test(expr)) throw new Error('invalid expression')
return evaluate(expr)
```

**Verification:** POST `require('child_process').exec('id')` to the affected endpoint and confirm HTTP 400 instead of OS command execution.

<a id="m-029"></a>

// libxmljs2 — disable external entities and network fetch.
const doc = libxmljs.parseXml(xmlBuf, { noent: false, nonet: true, recover: false })

// Per-IP + per-account rate limiting on auth endpoints.
import rateLimit from 'express-rate-limit'
app.use('/rest/user/login',

```typescript
// Use double-submit cookie / SameSite=Strict for state-changing routes.
app.use(csrf({ cookie: { sameSite: 'strict', httpOnly: true, secure: true } }))
```

**Verification:** Replay a state-changing POST without the CSRF token from a foreign origin and confirm 403.

<a id="m-017"></a>
#### M-017 — Hardcoded HMAC secret enables coupon forgery lib/insecurity.ts

**Addresses:**

- 🔴 [F-014](#f-014) — Hardcoded HMAC secret enables coupon forgery

**Priority:** P2 - This Sprint · **Effort:** Medium · **File:** `lib/insecurity.ts:44`

```typescript
// Load the RSA private key from an environment variable / KMS — never
// the source tree. Rotate the prior key and revoke outstanding tokens.
const privateKey = process.env.JWT_PRIVATE_KEY
if (!privateKey) throw new Error('JWT_PRIVATE_KEY not set')
const token = jwt.sign(claims, privateKey, { algorithm: 'RS256' })
```

**Verification:** `git grep -- 'BEGIN RSA PRIVATE KEY'` returns no matches in the working tree.

<a id="m-024"></a>
#### M-024 — NoSQL injection via \$where clause in product reviews showProductReviews.ts

**Addresses:**

- 🔴 [F-025](#f-025) — NoSQL injection via \$where clause in product reviews showProductReviews.ts

**Priority:** P2 - This Sprint · **Effort:** Medium · **File:** `routes/showProductReviews.ts:35`

```javascript
// Reject `$where` and operator keys from user-controlled input.
function safeQuery(filter) {
  for (const k of Object.keys(filter)) {
    if (k.startsWith('$')) throw new Error('operator not allowed')
  }
  return collection.find(filter)
}
```

**Verification:** Send `{ "$where": "sleep(5000) || true" }` and confirm the request is rejected (HTTP 400) within 100 ms instead of taking ~5 s.

<a id="m-031"></a>
#### M-031 — SSRF via profile image URL upload routes/profileImageUrlUpload.ts

**Addresses:**

- 🟠 [F-026](#f-026) — SSRF via profile image URL upload

**Priority:** P2 - This Sprint · **Effort:** Medium · **File:** `routes/profileImageUrlUpload.ts:1`

```typescript
// Allowlist external image hosts; block private IP ranges.
const ALLOWED_HOSTS = new Set(['images.example.com', 'cdn.example.com'])
const url = new URL(input)
if (!ALLOWED_HOSTS.has(url.hostname)) throw new Error('host not allowed')
if (/^(10\.|172\.(1[6-9]|2[0-9]|3[01])\.|192\.168\.|127\.)/.test(url.hostname))
  throw new Error('private range blocked')
```

**Verification:** POST a URL pointing at `http://169.254.169.254/` and confirm HTTP 400.

## 11. Out of Scope

The following items are **explicitly excluded** from this threat model. Findings against these areas should be tracked separately.

- Third-party hosted dependencies and SaaS endpoints
- Browser runtime vulnerabilities and end-user device security
- Operating system kernel and container runtime
- Underlying network infrastructure (DNS, BGP, ISP)
- Physical security of hosting facilities

<a id="appendix-a-vektor-taxonomy"></a>
## Appendix A — Vektor Taxonomy

This appendix defines the attacker-starting-position labels used in the Top Threats table and throughout [§8 Findings Register](#8-findings-register). Each label answers the question *what does the attacker need before the exploit begins?*

<a id="vektor-internet-anon"></a>
### Internet Anon

**Attacker position:** Unauthenticated attacker from the public internet · **Breach distance:** 1

**Preconditions:**

- Endpoint is reachable from the internet (no IP allowlist, no VPN)
- No authentication middleware blocks the request

**Typical CWEs:** [CWE-89](https://cwe.mitre.org/data/definitions/89.html) · [CWE-79](https://cwe.mitre.org/data/definitions/79.html) · [CWE-306](https://cwe.mitre.org/data/definitions/306.html) · [CWE-327](https://cwe.mitre.org/data/definitions/327.html) · [CWE-611](https://cwe.mitre.org/data/definitions/611.html) · [CWE-918](https://cwe.mitre.org/data/definitions/918.html)

**Typical OWASP Top 10:** A01:2021, A03:2021, A07:2021

<a id="vektor-internet-user"></a>
### Internet User

**Attacker position:** Any authenticated low-privilege user (valid JWT / session) · **Breach distance:** 2

**Preconditions:**

- Attacker has signed up or otherwise obtained a valid user session
- Endpoint is behind auth but not behind role/admin checks

**Typical CWEs:** [CWE-434](https://cwe.mitre.org/data/definitions/434.html) · [CWE-611](https://cwe.mitre.org/data/definitions/611.html) · [CWE-918](https://cwe.mitre.org/data/definitions/918.html) · [CWE-352](https://cwe.mitre.org/data/definitions/352.html) · [CWE-287](https://cwe.mitre.org/data/definitions/287.html)

**Typical OWASP Top 10:** A01:2021, A04:2021, A05:2021, A10:2021

<a id="vektor-internet-priv-user"></a>
### Internet Priv User

**Attacker position:** Authenticated admin-level user (JWT with admin role or equivalent) · **Breach distance:** 2

**Preconditions:**

- Attacker holds admin credentials or has elevated privileges
- Endpoint gated on admin role but still exploitable once reached

**Typical CWEs:** [CWE-862](https://cwe.mitre.org/data/definitions/862.html) · [CWE-79](https://cwe.mitre.org/data/definitions/79.html) · [CWE-94](https://cwe.mitre.org/data/definitions/94.html)

**Typical OWASP Top 10:** A01:2021

<a id="vektor-victim-required"></a>
### Victim-Required

**Attacker position:** Attacker needs victim interaction - social engineering, crafted link, or live session · **Breach distance:** 2

**Preconditions:**

- Victim must click a link, load a page, or have an active session
- Applies to XSS, CSRF, click-jacking, open redirect

**Typical CWEs:** [CWE-79](https://cwe.mitre.org/data/definitions/79.html) · [CWE-352](https://cwe.mitre.org/data/definitions/352.html) · [CWE-601](https://cwe.mitre.org/data/definitions/601.html) · [CWE-1021](https://cwe.mitre.org/data/definitions/1021.html)

**Typical OWASP Top 10:** A01:2021, A03:2021

<a id="vektor-build-time"></a>
### Build-Time

**Attacker position:** Attacker controls a build input - CI runner, dependency, base image, or external data fetched during build · **Breach distance:** 3

**Preconditions:**

- Compromise of a dependency, registry, or base image
- OR compromise of a CI runner with write access to artifacts

**Typical CWEs:** [CWE-506](https://cwe.mitre.org/data/definitions/506.html) · [CWE-829](https://cwe.mitre.org/data/definitions/829.html) · [CWE-1039](https://cwe.mitre.org/data/definitions/1039.html) · [CWE-1104](https://cwe.mitre.org/data/definitions/1104.html)

**Typical OWASP Top 10:** A08:2021

<a id="vektor-repo-read"></a>
### Repo-Read

**Attacker position:** Attacker gains read access to source repository (leaked clone, forked fork, insider, compromised developer workstation) · **Breach distance:** 3

**Preconditions:**

- Read access to the source tree at or after commit time
- No runtime exploit needed - the vulnerability is the content of the repo

**Typical CWEs:** [CWE-798](https://cwe.mitre.org/data/definitions/798.html) · [CWE-312](https://cwe.mitre.org/data/definitions/312.html) · [CWE-540](https://cwe.mitre.org/data/definitions/540.html)

**Typical OWASP Top 10:** A02:2021, A07:2021

<a id="vektor-n-a"></a>
### n/a

**Attacker position:** Architectural / meta-finding - no runtime entry point, the finding describes a design defect aggregating multiple code-level findings

**Preconditions:**

- Finding is AF-NNN (architectural) rather than F-NNN (code-level)
